name: Walks
preceding_figure_names: Basic In Place, Fallaway Rock, Link, Mooch, Reverse Whip, Whip
following_figure_names: Fallaway Throwaway, Change of Places Right to Left, Whip, Whip Throwaway
notes: What's shown above is a version common taught in classes, followed by a triple step (QaQ) and whip. Walks can start in PP. Variations: A more basic version ends at step 8, no more turns. Man could use a "merengue" action in place of single Q's: straighten R knee on second half of preceding step, making R hip to move back and to R; place LF diag fwd with part weight. Lady closes RF to LF, having turned 1/4 L on second half of preceding step; the other foot is similar. When using this variation, it can be curved slightly L.

steps.action: 1/8 to L on 1, No turn on 2 (part of Fallaway Rock)
LF diagonal forward to chassse LRL, ending in PP
RF forward and across in PP and CBMP to chassse RLR
LF diagonal forward turning Lady to L
RF forward and across in PP turning Lady to R
LF diagonal forward turning Lady to L
RF forward and across in PP turning Lady to R
LF diagonal forward turning Lady to L
RF forward and across in PP turning Lady to R
Side chasse RLR, end in PP | 1/4 to L over 3-4, start to turn R on 5
Fwd chassse LRL, end in PP | 1/4 to R over 5-6, start to turn L on 8
comments: Raise left arm straight up on side of left ear at end of step 8

steps.action: RF to side after turning 1/4 L on LF | start turning R at the end
comments: Start with straight up arm; elbow down forward, wrist at left side of face, hand behind neck at end

steps.action: LF forward in PP after turn 1/4 R on RF | start turning L at the end
comments: Elbow forward initially to avoid hitting the man; raise L arm straight and gradually place it on top of man's right shoulder over next 4 Q's

steps.action: RF to side after turning 1/4 L on LF | start turning R at the end
comments: Arm slowly getting down to man's right shoulder while twisting

steps.action: LF forward in PP after turning 1/4 R on RF | start turning L at the end
RF to side after turning 1/4 L on LF | start turning R at the end
LF forward in PP after turning 1/4 R on RF
comments: When to close or not depends on what follows next
